report.summary: UX audited 2 new learn guides from today's build cycle (Virtuals Protocol, GRASS Protocol DePIN), created 2 OG images (1200x630 SVG), fixed 6 broken OG image paths (.png → .svg), added semantic aria-labels to article elements, and added trust-building disclaimer sections with data source attribution.

sections.title: UX Improvements
items: Virtuals Protocol guide: Fixed OG image URL from .png to .svg across openGraph, twitter, and structured data (3 references)
GRASS Protocol guide: Fixed OG image URL from .png to .svg across openGraph, twitter, and structured data (3 references)
Virtuals Protocol guide: Added aria-label to <article> element for screen reader accessibility
GRASS Protocol guide: Added aria-label to <article> element for screen reader accessibility
Virtuals Protocol guide: Added educational disclaimer with data source attribution (CoinGecko, Virtuals Protocol docs)
GRASS Protocol guide: Added educational disclaimer with data source attribution and bandwidth privacy warning (CoinGecko, Grass Network docs)
Both pages already had good patterns: TOC with aria-label, Breadcrumb, BackToTop, semantic <article>, responsive clamp() typography, accessible table with overflow-x scroll

sections.title: Visuals Created
items: OG image: og-virtuals-protocol-guide.svg (1200x630) — indigo-cyan gradient, AI agent node network visualization, stats (18,000+ agents, $450M+ GDP, $1B+ mcap), category badge, brand footer
OG image: og-grass-protocol-depin-guide.svg (1200x630) — green-cyan gradient, DePIN data flow visualization, stats (8.5M MAU, 100+ TB daily, ~20 clients), dual category badges, brand footer

sections.title: UX Audit Findings (Deferred from previous cycles, still open)
items: Section spacing inconsistency across learn guides: some use 40px, others 44px, others 48px — recommend standardizing in a shared layout component
Focus styles missing on most pages — recommend adding global :focus-visible outlines in globals.css for all interactive elements
Heading hierarchy varies: some guides skip h3 level — recommend standardizing h1>h2>h3 across all guides
TOC links lack hover underline/focus ring — would benefit from a shared TOCNav component with built-in focus styles
